title: sideritis leucantha subsp. incana
intro: Sideritis leucantha subsp. incana, also known as White Mountain Sideritis, is a species of flowering plant in the genus Sideritis, native to the mountainous regions of the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Sideritis leucantha subsp. incana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and exhibit neuroprotective effects, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, and anxiety.
